title: Insurance underwriters
description: Review settled claims to determine that payments and settlements are made in accordance with company practices and procedures. Confer with legal counsel on claims requiring litigation. May also settle insurance claims. Appraise automobile or other vehicle damage to determine repair costs for insurance claim settlement. Prepare insurance forms to indicate repair cost or cost estimates and recommendations. May seek agreement with automotive repair shop on repair costs. Appraise and estimate the fair value of tangible personal or business property, such as jewelry, art, antiques, collectibles, and equipment. May also appraise land. (Based on 9 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Evaluate practicality of repair versus payment of market value of the vehicle before the accident.
Communicate with reinsurance brokers to obtain information necessary for processing claims.
Negotiate claim settlements or recommend litigation when settlement cannot be negotiated.
Create and maintain a database of completed appraisals and document physical characteristics of properties such as measurements and quality.
Inspect personal or business properties to investigate and assess damage, creating or reviewing property damage estimates.
Interview or correspond with claimants, witnesses, or relevant parties to determine claim settlements or reviews.
Verify and analyze data used in settling claims to ensure they are valid and made according to company practices and procedures.
Prepare insurance forms to indicate repair cost estimates and recommendations, and review repair cost estimates for agreement.
Prepare written reports that estimate property values and outline methods used, ensuring they meet appraisal standards.
Examine claims forms and records to determine insurance coverage and verify legal descriptions of properties.
Pay and process claims within designated authority level and maintain claim files, including records of settled claims.
Explain services or policies to clients, including debt management program rules, advantages, and disadvantages.

Attend company meetings to exchange product information and coordinate work activities.
Plan and prepare work schedules, and assign employees to specific duties as needed.
Monitor sales staff performance to ensure that goals are met and provide guidance on complex duties.
Listen to and resolve customer complaints regarding services, products, or personnel.
Teach courses or seminars on budgeting, personal finance management, or financial literacy topics.
